title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: SiYuan: Stored XSS to RCE via attribute-view cell rendering in genAVValueHTML()
description: ### Summary The attribute-view (database) cell renderer `genAVValueHTML` interpolates cell content raw in four of its branches: `text`, `url`, `phone`, and `mAsset`. A cell value like `` or `">` breaks out of its surrounding tag and runs arbitrary JavaScript in the renderer when the victim opens the block-attribute panel. On Electron desktop the renderer runs with `nodeIntegration:true`, so the XSS chains to host RCE via `require('chi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript via stored XSS, which can escalate to remote code execution (RCE) on Electron-based desktop applications. This can lead to full system compromise, data theft, or further exploitation of the host environment.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of the software if available, or apply any recommended mitigations provided by the vendor.
Disable or restrict the use of `nodeIntegration` in Electron applications to limit the potential for RCE.
Conduct a security audit of all applications using the vulnerable component to identify and mitigate potential exploitation vectors.

title: mcp-atlassian: Arbitrary file read via missing path validation in confluence_upload_attachment
description:
### Summary
`confluence_upload_attachment` passes `file_path` directly to `open(file_path, "rb")` with no path validation. Any authenticated MCP client — or an AI agent manipulated via prompt injection — can read any file the server process can access and exfiltrate it to Confluence as an attachment.

### Details
Root cause: `src/mcp_atlassian/confluence/attachments.py`, `_upload_attachment_direct()`:

```python
files = {"file": (filename, open(file_path, "rb"))}  # no validate_safe_path()
```

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users or manipulated AI agents to read arbitrary files on the server, potentially exposing sensitive data such as credentials, configuration files, or intellectual property. This could lead to data breaches, privilege escalation, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Implement path validation checks in the `confluence_upload_attachment` function to ensure only safe and intended file paths are accessed.
Restrict file system permissions for the server process to limit access to sensitive files.
Conduct a security review of other file-handling functions in the codebase to identify and mitigate similar vulnerabilities.

title: mcp-atlassian: Arbitrary server-side file read via attachment upload
description:
### Summary

A client that can invoke MCP tools can read **arbitrary files from the server host** and exfiltrate them as Atlassian attachments. The attachment-upload tools take a client-supplied `file_path` and `open()` it on the **server's** filesystem.

The upload tools are meant to attach a file from the **caller's** environment — the client supplies a path expecting it to refer to its own machine. Over a remote transport (HTTP/SSE) that path is instead resolved and read on the server, and th

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: SafeInstall agent guard shell parsing can miss raw package execution
description:
## Summary

SafeInstall CLI through 0.10.1 can fail to recognize some package-manager and registry-runner commands in its agent guard. Case-variant launcher names, leading file-descriptor redirections, and supported shell wrappers with options can cause a raw install command to receive no guard decision. Remote project scaffolding through package-manager create/init commands can also avoid the approval decision used for other registry runners.

## Impact

When the SafeInstall guard is installed

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: NotrinosERP: Authenticated arbitrary file upload leads to remote code execution via HRM employee "Documents" (doc_file)
description:
#### Summary
An authenticated user with the HR "Manage Employees" permission (`SA_EMPLOYEE`) can upload a file with an arbitrary extension through the employee **Documents** tab. The handler writes the raw client-supplied filename — extension intact — into a web served directory with no extension, MIME, or content validation, so a `.php` file is stored under the web root and executes as code, yielding remote code execution on the server.

#### Details
The document-upload branch in `hrm/manage/em

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: BabelDOC: Arbitrary Code Execution via CMap Pickle Deserialization in babeldoc/pdfminer/cmapdb.py
description:
## Arbitrary Code Execution via CMap Pickle Deserialization in babeldoc/pdfminer/cmapdb.py

### Summary

BabelDOC's vendored PDF parser (`babeldoc/pdfminer/cmapdb.py`) deserializes untrusted pickle data when loading CMap files. The `_load_data()` method strips only NUL bytes from a PDF-controlled CMap name, then passes it directly to `os.path.join()` and `pickle.loads()`. Because Python's `os.path.join()` discards all preceding path components when it encounters an absolute path segment, an atta

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54071.

title: File Browser: Command Injection via Authentication Hook Shell Substitution (Pre-Authentication RCE)
description:
## Overview

The Hook Authentication feature in File Browser allows administrators to delegate login verification to an external shell command. User-supplied credentials (username and password) are interpolated into this command string using `os.Expand` without sanitization. An **unauthenticated remote attacker** can inject shell metacharacters in the username or password field at the login screen, causing the server to execute arbitrary OS commands before any authentication takes place. This is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands on the server hosting the File Browser application. This could lead to full system compromise, data exfiltration, service disruption, or lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable the Hook Authentication feature in File Browser until a patched version is available.
Update to the latest patched version of File Browser as soon as it is released by the vendor.
Implement network-level protections, such as restricting access to the File Browser application to trusted IP ranges or using a VPN.

title: `exploration` was removed from crates.io for malicious code
description:
A method within the `exploration` crate attempted to download and execute a payload from a remote site.

The malicious crate had 1 version published on 2026-06-02, approximately 1 hour before removal, and had no evidence of actual usage. This crate had no dependencies on crates.io.

Rustsec to Kirill Boychenko from the [Socket Threat Research Team](https://socket.dev/) for reporting this crate.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The malicious crate 'exploration' attempted to download and execute a remote payload, potentially enabling remote code execution (RCE) or other malicious activities. While the crate was promptly removed and there is no evidence of usage, the incident highlights the risk of supply chain attacks in the Rust ecosystem.
recommended_actions: Audit all dependencies in your Rust projects to ensure no malicious or unverified crates are included, even if they are not directly used.
Implement automated tools to monitor and alert for suspicious or newly published crates in your dependency chain.
Educate development teams about supply chain risks and encourage the use of trusted sources and verified libraries.

title: Windmill: Resource-scoped API tokens can read script contents outside their allowed path via scripts/list_search
description:
### Summary

A resource-scoped API token can read script contents outside its allowed path scope via `GET /api/w/{workspace}/scripts/list_search`.

This appears to be a remaining variant of the scoped-token authorization class previously addressed for other endpoints. The route-level scope middleware validates the token domain/action, but does not enforce the resource/path segment of a scope. `scripts/list_search` then returns script `path` and `content` for scripts in the workspace without appl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows resource-scoped API tokens to bypass path restrictions and access sensitive script contents outside their intended scope. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure, potential intellectual property theft, or compromise of sensitive business logic.
recommended_actions: Implement stricter validation in the route-level scope middleware to enforce resource/path restrictions for all API endpoints.
Conduct a comprehensive security audit of all scoped-token authorization mechanisms to identify similar vulnerabilities.
Notify affected users and advise them to rotate API tokens and review access permissions as a precaution.

title: SiYuan: Stored XSS in Bazaar marketplace via package README event handlers
description:
## Summary

`renderPackageREADME` in `kernel/bazaar/readme.go` renders a Bazaar package README from Markdown to HTML with the lute engine and `SetSanitize(true)`. The lute sanitizer is an event-handler blocklist: `allowAttr` rejects only attribute names present in a fixed `eventAttrs` map copied from the w3schools legacy handler list.

That map omits modern event handlers. `onpointerover`, `onpointerdown`, `onauxclick`, `onbeforetoggle`, `onfocusin`, `onanimationstart`, and `ontransitionend` are

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in the context of a user's browser by exploiting unfiltered modern event handlers in package README files. This could lead to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation of users interacting with the Bazaar marketplace.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the `lute` sanitizer to include modern event handlers such as `onpointerover`, `onpointerdown`, `onauxclick`, `onbeforetoggle`, `onfocusin`, `onanimationstart`, and `ontransitionend` in its blocklist.
Conduct a security audit of all user-generated content rendering processes to ensure proper sanitization and protection against XSS vulnerabilities.
Notify users and developers of the vulnerability and encourage them to avoid interacting with untrusted README files until a patch is applied.

title: File Browser: Authentication Bypass via Proxy Auth Header Forgery
description:
## Summary

When FileBrowser is configured with proxy authentication (`auth.method=proxy`), any unauthenticated attacker who can reach the server directly can impersonate **any user - including admin** - by sending a single forged HTTP header. No credentials are required. Additionally, specifying a non-existent username causes the server to **automatically create a new user account**, providing an account creation primitive with no authorization.

**This is an already known issue that has been d

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54089.

title: SiYuan: Unauthenticated Admin API Access via Blanket chrome-extension:// Origin Allowlist
description:
## Summary

SiYuan Note's kernel HTTP server unconditionally trusts all `chrome-extension://` origins, granting `RoleAdministrator` access to every installed browser extension without any authentication. Combined with the default empty `AccessAuthCode` on desktop installs, any Chrome/Chromium extension -- including a compromised legitimate extension via supply chain attack -- can make fully authenticated admin API calls to the SiYuan kernel at `127.0.0.1:6806`, enabling data exfiltration, stored

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54069.

title: SiYuan: Unauthenticated SQLite Data Exfiltration via Template Injection in /api/icon/getDynamicIcon
description:
### Summary

The `/api/icon/getDynamicIcon` endpoint is explicitly excluded from authentication in SiYuan's kernel router (`router.go`, "不需要鉴权" -- no auth needed). When called with `type=8` and a valid block `id` parameter, this endpoint invokes `RenderDynamicIconContentTemplate`, which executes a Go template that includes the `querySQL` and `queryBlocks` functions. These functions run arbitrary SELECT statements against the SiYuan SQLite database. An unauthenticated network-adjacent attacker wh

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54068.

title: CredSweeper: Recursive archive size-limit bypass in deep scanner allows crafted compressed inputs to exhaust resources
description:
### Summary
CredSweeper's deep scanner does not enforce `recursive_limit_size` as a hard limit. Several recursive scanners fully decompress or fully read attacker-controlled content before the remaining budget is validated, and `AbstractScanner.recursive_scan()` continues processing even when the residual budget is already negative.

This allows a crafted archive to bypass the intended recursive zip-bomb protection and force excessive memory / CPU consumption when deep scanning is enabled (`--de

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in CredSweeper's deep scanner allows attackers to craft malicious compressed files that bypass recursive size limits, leading to potential resource exhaustion (memory/CPU). This could result in denial-of-service (DoS) conditions or degraded performance for systems using the tool.
recommended_actions: Implement stricter validation of recursive size limits in CredSweeper's deep scanner to ensure the budget is enforced before processing content.
Apply rate-limiting or resource caps to prevent excessive resource consumption during deep scanning.
Monitor and update to the latest version of CredSweeper once a patch addressing this issue is released.

title: Excelize: Unbounded Row Index Allocation in Worksheet Parser (checkSheet OOM/Panic DoS)
description:
## Unbounded Row Index Allocation in Worksheet Parser (checkSheet OOM/Panic DoS)

### Summary
The `checkSheet()` function in `github.com/xuri/excelize/v2` uses an attacker-controlled `<row r="N">` XML attribute value directly as the length argument to `make([]xlsxRow, row)` without validating it against the Excel row limit (`TotalRows = 1,048,576`). A specially crafted XLSX file can trigger two denial-of-service variants: (A) an out-of-memory process kill when `r=2147483647` forces a ~16 GB allo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54063.

title: Authorizer: Unvalidated redirect_uri in /authorize leaks OAuth2 tokens to attacker-controlled URL
description:
## Summary

The `/authorize` endpoint accepts any `redirect_uri` without validating it against `AllowedOrigins`. When `response_type=token` or `response_type=id_token`, the server appends `access_token`, `id_token`, and `refresh_token` as query parameters and issues a 302 redirect to the attacker-supplied URL. An unauthenticated attacker can obtain the required `client_id` from the public `/graphql?query={meta{client_id}}` endpoint.

Partial fix was applied in v2.0.1 to other handlers (`oauth_lo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54072.

title: SiYuan: Stored XSS to RCE via CSS-snippet <style> breakout in renderSnippet()
description: ### Summary A CSS snippet body containing `` breaks out of its surrounding `` runs arbitrary JavaScript in the renderer. On Electron desktop builds the renderer runs with `nodeIntegration:true`, so `require('child_process')` is reachable from the injected handler and the XSS chains to host RCE. Snippets sync via the workspace repository, so an attacker with writ

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54067.

title: SiYuan: Path Traversal via Double URL Encoding in /assets/*path (publish mode arbitrary   file─read), Incomplete fix of CVE-2026-41894
description:
## Summary
  The patch for CVE-2026-41894 ("Path Traversal via Double URL Encoding") sanitized the `/export/` route but the
  **identical root cause remains in the `/assets/*path` route**. In publish mode (anonymous read-only HTTP endpoint,
  default port 6808), an unauthenticated remote attacker can read arbitrary files inside `WorkspaceDir` — including
  `conf/conf.json` (which contains the `AccessAuthCode` SHA256 hash, API token, and sync keys), `temp/siyuan.db`,
  `temp/blocktree.db`, and `s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to exploit a path traversal flaw via double URL encoding in the '/assets/*path' route. This can lead to the exposure of sensitive files, including configuration files and database files, which may contain API tokens, authentication hashes, and other critical data. If exploited, this could result in unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential compromise of the affected system.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply a patch or update to address the incomplete fix for CVE-2026-41894 and sanitize the '/assets/*path' route to prevent path traversal attacks.
Restrict access to the publish mode endpoint (default port 6808) by implementing network-level access controls, such as IP whitelisting or VPN requirements.
Monitor logs for unusual access patterns or unauthorized file access attempts, and consider rotating API tokens and authentication keys as a precaution.

title: Deloitte AI Assist for Customer disclosed some configuration information through public-facing...
description: Deloitte AI Assist for Customer disclosed some configuration information through public-facing API endpoints that accepted unauthenticated requests. This information could reduce an attacker’s reconnaissance effort. On 2026-03-25, AI Assist for Customer restricted network access and enforced authentication for the previously exposed endpoints.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The disclosure of configuration information through unauthenticated API endpoints could allow attackers to gain insights into system architecture, potentially aiding in targeted attacks or exploitation. While the issue has been mitigated, the exposed data may still pose a residual risk if attackers have already accessed it.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough audit of all public-facing API endpoints to ensure proper authentication and access controls are enforced.
Analyze logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts during the exposure period and assess potential data leakage.
Educate development teams on secure API design practices to prevent similar issues in the future.

title: Deloitte AI Assist for Customer exposed unauthenticated API endpoints that allowed an attacker...
description: Deloitte AI Assist for Customer exposed unauthenticated API endpoints that allowed an attacker with knowledge of additional parameters to read from or inject content into the retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) corpus. On 2026-03-25, AI Assist for Customer restricted network access and enforced authentication for the previously exposed endpoints.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The exposed unauthenticated API endpoints could allow attackers to manipulate or extract sensitive data from the retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) corpus, potentially leading to data breaches, misinformation, or unauthorized access to proprietary information.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough audit of all API endpoints to ensure proper authentication and access controls are in place.
Implement monitoring and logging for API usage to detect and respond to any suspicious activity.
Educate development teams on secure API design practices to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

title: CVE-2026-13722 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the
description: WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the backup/restore feature. An authenticated administrator can exploit this vulnerability to install a tampered firmware image.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2025.6.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13722.

description: An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS wgagent process could allow an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted requests to the Management Web UI.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 12.1 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13384.

title: CVE-2026-13377 - Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerab
description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS SIP Proxy module allows Stored XSS. This vulnerability is an additional unmitigated attack path for CVE-2025-6947.

This issue affects Fireware OS 12.0 up to and including 12.12, 12.5 up to and including 12.5.18, and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13377.

description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS spamBlocker module allows Stored XSS. This vulnerability is an additional unmitigated attack path for CVE-2025-1071.

This issue affects Fireware OS 12.0 up to and including 12.12, 12.5 up to and including 12.5.18, and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

description:

description:

title: CVE-2026-13371 - An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management
description: An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management Web UI by sending malformed or crafted data to the put_data endpoint, which performs unsafe deserialization of the attacker-supplied input.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13371.

title: CVE-2026-13368 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP a
description:
WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP authentication for the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2. A remote unauthenticated attacker could exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary code in the context of the iked process on Fireboxes that have a Mobile VPN with IKEv2 configured to use an external LDAP authentication server.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13368.

title: CVE-2026-13084 - A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticate
description:
A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition by sending specially crafted IKEv2 messages. This vulnerability affects both the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2 and the Branch Office VPN using IKEv2 when configured with a dynamic gateway peer.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.10.2 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13084.

title: CVE-2026-13079 - A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows
description:
A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows allows a local attacker to escalate their privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM on the machine where the client is installed.

This issue affects the Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13079.

title: CVE-2026-13054 - A path traversal vulnerability in the WatchGuard Fireware OS Management Web UI allows a privileged a
description:
A path traversal vulnerability in the WatchGuard Fireware OS Management Web UI allows a privileged authenticated attacker to write arbitrary files on the Firebox's filesystem.




This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13054.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13053.

description: An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS networkd process could allow an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted requests to the Management Web UI.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.8 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.
